other_observers: Kara Lewantowicz Bob Gooycoolea; Kara Lewantowicz found the bird but called it a Common Loon. I identified it. Later both she and Bob Gooycoolea agreed with the identification as it swam closer to us.
description: This bird is a large loon with a massive straw-colored bill. It had a clean white face. The back was pale and checkered. The bird dove frequently. The bird, when not diving, held its bill at an upward angle to the water. Culmen angled upwards. Auricular patch visible, but not in the photo. Nape light gray. Large blocky head.Behaviors: Frequent diving for crawfish.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
